summary: Elder Stevenson teaches that because every soul is precious to Heavenly Father, disciples of Jesus Christ should minister with careful, Christlike attention—especially to those assigned to us and to the rising generation—so none are forgotten, neglected, or lost.
haiku:
Precious souls journey
Christlike hands remember all
Homeward, safely loved
key_points: Heavenly Father knows, loves, and claims each of His children as precious and never forgotten.
Ministering brothers and sisters are sacred stewards who watch over, strengthen, and nourish others through small acts of Christlike care.
The rising generation needs intentional teaching, refuge, and encouragement to know Jesus Christ, hear the Holy Ghost, and make covenants.
scriptures_and_gospel_principles: Doctrine and Covenants 20:53 — watching over Church members and being with and strengthening them
Mosiah 18:8–10 — bearing one another’s burdens and standing as witnesses of God
Doctrine and Covenants 68:25–28 — parents teaching children faith, repentance, baptism, and the Holy Ghost
Doctrine and Covenants 93:40 — bringing up children in light and truth
2 Nephi 25:26 — teaching children to look to Christ for remission of sins
The doctrine of divine identity: each person is a beloved child of Heavenly Parents
Ministering as Christlike caring through love, service, comfort, and covenant support
President Russell M. Nelson’s teachings on learning to hear the Holy Ghost
President Dieter F. Uchtdorf’s promise about teaching, nurturing, and trusting God’s miracles
questions_to_ponder: Who has Heavenly Father entrusted to my care right now?
What small improvement could make my ministering more thoughtful, consistent, and Christlike?
How can I help someone feel remembered rather than forgotten, neglected, or lost?
What can I do to better strengthen children, youth, or young adults in their faith in Jesus Christ?
How am I helping the rising generation learn to recognize and follow the Holy Ghost?
